name: OpenEtruscan
description: Open-source digital corpus platform for Etruscan epigraphy. Unifies the Etruscan epigraphic record into a computationally accessible corpus (6,633 inscriptions), publishes it as Linked Open Data, and ships a multilingual encoder (LaBSE + XLM-R with Etruscan-side LoRA) that places Etruscan and Latin lexicons in a shared vector space. Includes a frozen reference benchmark (rosetta-eval-v1) with random, Levenshtein, and model baselines plus a reproducibility manifest.

isPartOf.name: Pelagios Network
isPartOf.description: International network of linked open data for the ancient world. OpenEtruscan contributes to the People, Place, and Registry working groups; aligns findspots with Pleiades and prosopography with the Pelagios People graph.

referencePublication.name: OpenEtruscan corpus v1 — unified Etruscan inscriptions
referencePublication.description: ML-ready cleaned corpus of 6,567 unified Etruscan inscriptions. Derived from Larth Dataset (Vico & Spanakis 2023) and CIE Vol. I.
